title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: Full test suite: Single test file context: Generate documentation roxygen: Full package check (runs R CMD CHECK equivalent): Code formatting: Uses air formatter configured air.toml (100 char line width, 4-space indent). Run edits air format . main directory.
code: devtools::test() devtools::test(filter = "schema")     # test-schema.R devtools::test(filter = "validate")   # test-validate.R devtools::document() devtools::check()

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: schemar developer-focused package creating structured data frame subtypes. three core layers:

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: sch_schema(...) – Top-level function defines structured data frame schema. Takes named column specifications (type constructors) optional description. Type Constructors – sch_numeric(), sch_integer(), sch_character(), sch_factor(), sch_date(), sch_datetime(), sch_logical(), sch_inherits(), sch_list_of(), sch_custom(). Nesting – sch_nest() defines nested column groups (stored flat list-columns). Supports .keys key-value validation. columns – sch_others() permits additional unlisted columns. Type registry – type_fns list maps type names check/msg/coerce functions. Custom types added way.

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: sch_validate(schema, data, check=c("names","types","distinct","nesting"), ...) – Core validation function. "names" → validate_names() – missing required columns, extra columns, missing inner columns nests "types" → validate_types_missing() – type mismatches, NAs missing=FALSE "distinct" → validate_distinct() – duplicate values distinct=TRUE columns (NAs excluded per semantics) "nesting" → validate_nests() – nested schema structure, inner validation, key consistency Error handling – Collects issues raising single sch_validation_error attached $issues list.

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: print.sch_schema() – Pretty-prints schema indentation nesting levels, type descriptions, constraint flags. format.sch_schema() – Returns named character vector internal consumption.

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: Every type type_fns structure: Important: Check functions must wrap non-logical results isTRUE() validation avoid crashes NA returns.
code: type_name = list(     check = function(x, type) { ... },      # Returns TRUE/FALSE; must handle NA gracefully     msg = function(type) { ... },           # Returns human-readable type description     coerce = function(x, type) { ... }      # Attempts coercion (may fail) )

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: List elements – metadata like type, levels, bounds, closed (accessed via $) Attributes – constraints like required, missing, distinct, desc (accessed via attr()) List elements – cols (column definitions), keys (nests)

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: Flat nest (unnamed sch_nest()) – columns stored side--side data frame; outer columns collapse unique rows checking distinctness. Named nest – columns stored list-column data frames; element validated independently. Keys (.keys = c("col1", "col2")) – unique combinations columns must identical across groups defined outer columns (flat nests) across list elements (named nests). Group-logic – Inner distinct=TRUE columns checked per outer group (outer distinct/group columns exist).

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: Regular columns – NAs explicitly allowed/forbidden per missing attribute. List columns – NULL list elements treated “missing” (analogous NA). Distinct checking – NAs/NULLs excluded uniqueness checks missing=TRUE (“duplicate NA” error). Custom checks – Must handle NAs gracefully; check returns NA, ’s treated type failure, crash.

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: columns must named except sch_others() (name) unnamed sch_nest() (flat nesting). Nested nests (nests inside nests) allowed unnamed nests inside parent nest.

title: Copilot Instructions for schemar
text: sch_validate() modularization – check phase can run independently (useful performance; missing-column detection now happens validate_names(), validate_types_missing()). Recursive nesting – validate_names(), validate_types_missing(), validate_distinct(), validate_nests() accept path (column path error reporting) call recursively nested columns. vctrs integration – Uses vctrs::vec_unique_count(), vec_group_loc(), vec_slice() vectorized operations; handles NA semantics correctly. Error collection – validators return list issues (possibly empty); issues collected, printed bullet list cli::cli_abort().

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: Provides developer-focused helper functions S3 classes ease creation structured subtypes data frames require certain columns types present, preserve special attributes common data frame manipulations.

what: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: Defines structure single 'observation' structured data frame. column type restrictions may required optional. Schemas support nesting relationships.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(..., .desc = NULL, .relationships = NULL)  sch_others()  sch_multiple(   name,   type,   desc = NULL,   required = TRUE,   check = NULL,   msg = NULL,   coerce = NULL )  sch_nest(..., .desc = NULL)  sch_numeric(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_integer(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_logical(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_character(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_factor(   desc = NULL,   levels,   strict = TRUE,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_date(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.Date(-Inf), as.Date(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_datetime(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.POSIXct(-Inf), as.POSIXct(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_inherits(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_list_of(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_custom(   name,   desc = NULL,   check,   msg,   coerce,   ...,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: ... Column specifications, form col_name = col_type pairs, col_type call column type constructor listed , sch_numeric(). Every type must kind vector, .e., vctrs::obj_is_vector() must return TRUE. columns must named, except sch_others(), described , sch_multiple(), describes group columns sharing type. named sch_nest() describes columns stored nested data frame. special function sch_others() indicates preferred location columns explicitly mentioned schema. sch_others() appears, columns allowed. Trailing commas permitted. .relationships optional one-sided formula describing structural relationships values different columns. Formulas can involve named arguments .... Use * signify crossed levels, verify combinations exist, / signify nested levels, + create compound keys (bundling columns single identifier). See examples . name name custom type. type column type constructor (e.g. sch_numeric()) specifying expected type every column group. desc, .desc description column consumers schema. type contraints described separately need included description.  example "age", description might "Age patient years", "Non-negative integer representing age patient years". required TRUE (default), group entry sch_groups must contain least one column name. FALSE, empty character vector entry also accepted. check two-argument function checks whether object satisfies type. first argument object check, second full type specification. msg one-argument function generates descriptive message type passed type object . end period. coerce two-argument function attempts coerce object type. first argument object coerce, second full type specification. bounds Length-two vector c(min, max) specifying allowed range values. closed Length-two logical vector specifying whether bounds closed (inclusive) open (exclusive). missing TRUE, column may contain missing values. Otherwise, missing values result error. distinct TRUE, column must contain duplicate values (accounting nesting structure). levels character vector factor levels. strict TRUE, factors specified levels accepted. FALSE, character vectors specified levels also accepted. class character vector class names.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: object class sch_schema,

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: sch_others(): placeholder non-required columns schema. sch_multiple(): group multiple columns sharing type. group identified name, must appear entry sch_groups attribute data frame validated. entry character vector column names belong group. Optionally accepts cross-column check, msg, coerce functions applied entire group per-column type checks pass. must provided together . sch_multiple() must unnamed sch_schema() call. Per-column constraints missing distinct set inner type argument. sch_nest(): set columns stored nested list-column data frames. Must given name outer sch_schema(). sch_numeric(): numeric vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_integer(): integer vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_logical(): logical vector. sch_character(): character vector. sch_factor(): factor specified levels. sch_date(): Date vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_datetime(): POSIXct vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_inherits(): list-column whose elements satisfy inherits(_, class). sch_list_of(): vector satisfying inherits(_, class). sch_custom(): custom type defined user-provided check, type message, coercion functions. Additional named values stored along type specification may passed via ... available check, message, coercion function elements type argument.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(     .desc = "Student data",     age = sch_integer("Age in years", bounds = c(0, 130)),     birthday = sch_date("Date of birth", required = FALSE),     height = sch_numeric(         "Height in inches",         bounds = c(0, 108),         closed = c(FALSE, TRUE)     ),     teacher = sch_factor(levels = c("Jones", "Smith", "Hernandez")),     enrolled = sch_logical(missing = FALSE),     sch_others() ) #> Student data #> A schema with 4 required elements: #>      age  Age in years: An integer vector with values in [0, 130]. #> birthday  (optional) Date of birth: A date vector. #>   height  Height in inches: A numeric vector with values in (0, 108]. #>  teacher  A factor; one of Jones, Smith, or Hernandez. #> enrolled  A logical vector. No NAs allowed. #>      ...  Other columns  sch_schema(     .desc = "MCMC draws",     .relationships = ~ chain * draw * parameter,     chain = sch_integer("Chain number"),     draw = sch_integer("Draw number", bounds = c(1, Inf), closed = c(TRUE, FALSE)),     parameter = sch_factor("Parameter name", levels = c("mu", "sigma", "log_lik")),     value = sch_numeric("Parameter value") ) #> MCMC draws #> A schema with 4 required elements: #>     chain  Chain number: An integer vector. #>      draw  Draw number: An integer vector with values in [1, Inf). #> parameter  Parameter name: A factor; one of mu, sigma, or log_lik. #>     value  Parameter value: A numeric vector. #> Relationships: chain × draw × parameter  sch_custom(    name = "even",    check = function(x, type) is.integer(x) && all(x %% 2 == 0),    msg = function(type) "vector of even integers",    coerce = function(x, type) (as.integer(x) %/% 2) * 2 ) #> A vector of even integers

what: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: Checks data frame conforms schema, validating column presence, types, missing values, uniqueness constraints, nesting structure.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: sch_validate(   schema,   data,   check = c("names", "types", "distinct", "relationships"),   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: schema schema object created sch_schema(). data data frame validate. check character vector specifying checks perform. default runs checks. Possible values: "names": check missing required columns unexpected extra columns. "types": check column types missing-value (NA) constraints. "distinct": check uniqueness constraints columns marked distinct = TRUE. Relatively expensive. "relationships": validate relationship formulas (primary-key uniqueness crossing/nesting completeness). Relatively expensive. call environment call used error reporting, passed rlang::abort(). Useful wrapping sch_validate() inside another function error points right place.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: data, invisibly, validation succeeds. Otherwise, error class sch_validation_error raised formatted summary issues found.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: # Basic validation: valid data passes silently schema <- sch_schema(     id = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE),     name = sch_character(missing = FALSE),     age = sch_numeric(required = FALSE) ) df <- data.frame(id = 1:3, name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"), age = c(25, NA, 30)) sch_validate(schema, df)  # Invalid, throw validation errors if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # missing required columns sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = 1:2))  # type constrains not satisfied sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = c(1L, 1L), name = c("Alice", NA))) } # }
